open_items.title: Confirm the Tuesday DCC packet renders in the live app
open_items.summary: The May 19 file replaced a skeleton. Open DCC and confirm today's calendar, source health, and remaining-day schedule render correctly.
open_items.notes: Done when the dashboard shows May 19, not the stale May 14 packet or an empty skeleton.

open_items.title: Close the Sweep Suite live-reader gap
open_items.summary: The executable Sweep Suite harness passed, but its live Calendar/Gmail/Slack readers are still specs. Today's live sweep had to be done manually through connectors.
open_items.notes: Harness result: pass. Writer idempotency passed with success then skipped duplicates. Next step is implementing or wiring live reader scripts.

open_items.title: Piedmont Natural Gas bill ready - $8.00 due June 9
open_items.summary: Utility bill surfaced in the Gmail sweep. Due date is June 9, 2026.

open_items.title: Greenville Water bill ready
open_items.summary: Water bill notification surfaced in the Gmail sweep. Amount was not visible in the email snippet, so review the portal or bill before closeout.

Best,

source_health.summary: Harness passed: 8 skills present, writer idempotency succeeded, duplicate writes skipped correctly.
Calendar scanned for May 19. Found all-day Griffin House, Crossroads Coffee, and Southside Tennis.
Unread/starred search returned a noisy batch. Actionable items were bills/statements; promotions and newsletters were ignored.

Slack web search scanned recent DMs, mentions, and Offers channels. Actionable items: zero-match AMP alert, low-investor deal sourcing decision, Coty referral-progress ping, and one low-priority manufactured-home guidance thread.

notifications.title: Tuesday sweep completed across calendar, Gmail, and Slack web
notifications.body: Sweep Suite harness passed. Calendar scanned successfully. Gmail surfaced utility/admin items plus noise. Slack web search found work-responsibility signals in Offers channels and HubSpot Breeze DMs.

assessment.day_theme: Use the open home-work block to make DCC/Sweep Suite more trustworthy, batch admin once, then protect tennis.
hard_commitments: All day: Work from the Griffin House
must_do: Confirm the May 19 DCC packet renders in the live app.
Turn today's manual sweep into a concrete Sweep Suite live-reader next step.
Handle Slack work-responsibility checks: 37 Copper Leaf zero-match alert, low-investor deal sourcing decision, and Coty referral agreement progress.
Handle the utility/admin Gmail batch without letting it sprawl.
Close out before the tennis buffer so Wednesday starts cleanly.
nice_to_do: Archive or label obvious Gmail noise after the admin batch.
Decide whether the unsolicited daily-command-center outreach deserves any response.
Review YouTube Music trial/cancellation only if subscriptions are already on your mind.
risks: Slack was not swept, so avoid treating communications as fully clear.
Gmail is noisy enough that broad unread/starred should stay a triage source, not a priority source.
Tennis compresses the evening; closeout needs to happen before 17:00.

timeline.label: Crossroads Coffee
timeline.description: Crossroads 4 Coffee and Tea, 112 E Standing Springs Rd, Simpsonville, SC 29680

timeline.label: DCC packet render check
timeline.description: Open the local DCC and verify it is showing the May 19 packet, not the old May 14 packet or a skeleton.
timeline.notes: Checklist: confirm the date; confirm Crossroads Coffee is marked complete; confirm Southside Tennis appears at 18:30; confirm the source-health notification shows Slack as blocked; click one task card and verify notes render. Done when the live dashboard can be used for the rest of the day.

timeline.label: Sweep Suite live-reader gap
timeline.description: Convert today's manual sweep path into the next concrete reader implementation step.
timeline.notes: Checklist: record that sweep_suite_check.py passes but readers are specs; choose whether to script calendar first or Gmail filtering first; preserve Slack as blocked until tools are exposed; write the next small implementation task so future sweeps are less manual. Done when there is a specific next PR-sized step, not just a vague automation wish.

timeline.label: Daily Command Center focus block
timeline.description: Use one clean block for the highest-leverage DCC fix or planning task that emerges from the render check.
timeline.notes: Pick one: DCC state-source reliability, sweep packet rendering polish, or reader/writer integration. Avoid broad refactors. Done when one user-visible reliability issue is either fixed, clearly scoped, or intentionally parked.

timeline.label: Lunch / reset
timeline.description: Step away before the afternoon block.

timeline.label: Admin and bills batch
timeline.description: Quickly review the utility/statement emails surfaced by Gmail and decide pay, schedule, archive, or defer.
timeline.notes: Fast batch, not a big admin project. Items: Duke Energy $29.77 due June 15; Piedmont Natural Gas $8.00 due June 9; Greenville Water bill amount needs portal review; Pennymac monthly statement; YouTube Music trial/cancellation only if relevant. Done when each item has a next action or is archived.

timeline.label: Document today's sweep routine
timeline.description: Write down the exact source order, query filters, and source-confidence rules used for today's plan.
timeline.notes: Source order: Sweep Suite harness, Calendar May 19, Gmail unread/starred since last watermark with noisy senders filtered, DCC carryover files, local block DB sanity check. Source gaps: Slack unavailable, DCC day files were skeletons, local block DB was stale. Done when future you can recreate this packet without reading this chat.

timeline.label: One carried project block
timeline.description: Choose one stale carryover project and make a bounded move: Audit Angel QA, HYHH backup-first follow-through, or the DCC reliability item from this morning.
timeline.notes: Use this as a decision-quality block, not a second planning session. Done means one repo/task is left in a more intentional state than it started.

timeline.label: Closeout and Wednesday inputs
timeline.description: Mark actuals, park unfinished work honestly, and set up the next morning before tennis takes over.
timeline.notes: Checklist: update completed/skipped blocks; record whether Slack remained unswept; move unfinished DCC/Sweep Suite work to Wednesday or a later bucket; jot the next admin due-date action; protect tennis buffer. Done when Wednesday has the first next step.

timeline.label: Dinner / tennis buffer
timeline.description: Food, gear, drive, and decompression before Southside Tennis.

timeline.label: Southside Tennis
timeline.description: Southside Park, 417 Baldwin Rd, Simpsonville, SC 29680
